question_text:
Backspace String Compare

Given two strings s and t, return true if they are equal when both are typed into empty text editors. '#' means a backspace character.

Note that after backspacing an empty text, the text will continue empty.
input_format: First line: string s. Second line: string t.
output_format: Print "true" if the two strings are equal after processing, else "false".
constraints: s and t only contain lowercase letters and '#' characters.
explanation: The optimal O(N) time, O(1) space solution uses two pointers that traverse from the right end of each string. We maintain a "skip" counter for each string: when we see a '#', we increment the skip count; when we see a letter and skip > 0, we skip it (decrement skip); when we see a letter and skip == 0, this is the effective character. We compare the effective characters from both strings simultaneously. If at any point they differ, return false. If both pointers reach the start without mismatch, return true.
